rationale: The cited paper is a marketing-methods recommender model for experience products (hybrid collaborative filtering + attribute-based utility), which is directly adjacent to the citing paper’s core task of preference/link prediction in music recommendation settings. The citing note explicitly positions its contribution against prior recommender and dyadic preference-model approaches, so this reference is plausibly methodological/foundational rather than a purely incidental mention. With this topical and methodological alignment, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
